perturbation.original_text: You recognize and acknowledge the confidential and non-public nature of the Evaluation Materials, the competitive value of the Evaluation Materials, and the damage that could result to the Company if the Evaluation Materials were used or disclosed except as authorized by this Agreement.
perturbation.changed_text: You recognize and acknowledge the private and non-released nature of the Evaluation Materials, the commercial value of the Evaluation Materials, and the damage that could result to the Meridian Point if the Evaluation Materials were used or disclosed except as authorized by this Agreement.
perturbation.explanation: Switching "confidential" to "private", "non-public" to "non-released", "competitive" to "commercial", and "Company" to "Meridian Point" shifts the emphasis. 'Private' implies a personal aspect, while 'confidential' is more legal/business-oriented. 'Non-released' is less legally precise than 'non-public'. 'Commercial value' is broader than 'competitive value'. This introduces ambiguity as to what standards apply. Later in the document, referencing the Company, creates confusion with these different meanings. Creates an In-Text Contradiction
perturbation.contradicted_text: Although the Company has endeavored to include in the Evaluation Materials information which it believes to be relevant for the purpose of your investigation, you acknowledge and agree that none of the Company or the Company's representatives or agents is making any representation or warranty, expressed or implied, as to the accuracy or completeness of the Evaluation Materials, and none of the Company or the Company's other representatives or agents, nor any of their respective officers, directors, employees, representatives, stockholders, owners, affiliates, advisors or agents, will have any liability to you or any other person resulting from the use of Evaluation Materials by you or any of your representatives. Only those representations or warranties that are made to you in a formal written agreement involving your investment in or acquisition of all or part of the assets or equity of the Company, when, as, and if it is executed, and subject to such limitations and restrictions as may be specified in such agreement, will have any legal effect.
perturbation.justification: The changed text replaces "Company" with "Meridian Point". The contradicted text refers to "the Company" multiple times regarding the Evaluation Materials. This creates a direct conflict because the changed text reassigns the owner/originator of the Evaluation Materials from "the Company" to "Meridian Point", leading to ambiguity and contradiction about which entity is responsible and whose information is being evaluated. It is a misaligned terminology per the Perturbation Definition and an In-Text Contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: The term "Evaluation Materials" includes information furnished to you orally or in writing (whatever the form or storage medium) or gathered by inspection regarding the Company, and regardless of whether such information is specifically identified as "confidential".
perturbation.changed_text: The term "Evaluation Data" includes information furnished to you orally or in writing (whatever the form or storage medium) or gathered by inspection regarding the Meridian Point, and regardless of whether such information is specifically identified as "private".
perturbation.explanation: Changing "Evaluation Materials" to "Evaluation Data" and "confidential" to "private" alters the defined term. 'Data' is more technical than 'Materials', which has a broader scope. Changing confidential for private, changes the impact to the agreement terms, specially in the "Return of Documents". Creates an In-Text Contradiction
perturbation.contradicted_text: Upon the Company's request, you shall promptly deliver to the Company or destroy all written Evaluation Materials and any other written materials without retaining, in whole or in part, any copies, extracts or other reproductions (whatever the form or storage medium) of such materials, and shall certify the destruction of such materials in writing to the Company.
perturbation.justification: The change from "Evaluation Materials" to "Evaluation Data" narrows the scope of what must be returned or destroyed. 'Data' implies a more technical or specific subset of 'Materials'. Furthermore, the change from 'confidential' to 'private' could have implications, as confidential information may encompass a broader range than just private information. This creates an in-text contradiction because the contradicted text requires the return of all "Evaluation Materials", but the changed definition only covers a subset (Evaluation Data), potentially allowing the recipient to retain materials that were originally intended to be returned. This aligns with the 'Misaligned Terminology - In Text Contradiction' definition, as the terms are used interchangeably without proper definition, leading to conflicting obligations regarding the return of materials.

perturbation.original_text: You will not use any of the Evaluation Materials for any purpose other than the exclusive purpose of evaluating a Possible Transaction.
perturbation.changed_text: You will not utilize any of the Evaluation Data for any purpose other than the single purpose of assessing a Future Deal.
perturbation.explanation: Switching "Evaluation Materials" to "Evaluation Data", "purpose" to "single purpose", "evaluating" to "assessing", and "Possible Transaction" to "Future Deal" makes the restriction ambiguous. 'Utilize' is less common in legal contexts than 'use'. 'Single purpose' isn't as strong as 'exclusive purpose'. 'Assessing' and 'Future Deal' are less formal. Now, in "No representation or Warranty", it refers to the Possible Transaction, and creates confusion due to the change of terminology in this section. Creates an In-Text Contradiction
perturbation.contradicted_text: Although the Company has endeavored to include in the Evaluation Materials information which it believes to be relevant for the purpose of your investigation, you acknowledge and agree that none of the Company or the Company's representatives or agents is making any representation or warranty, expressed or implied, as to the accuracy or completeness of the Evaluation Materials, and none of the Company or the Company's other representatives or agents, nor any of their respective officers, directors, employees, representatives, stockholders, owners, affiliates, advisors or agents, will have any liability to you or any other person resulting from the use of Evaluation Materials by you or any of your representatives. Only those representations or warranties that are made to you in a formal written agreement involving your investment in or acquisition of all or part of the assets or equity of the Company, when, as, and if it is executed, and subject to such limitations and restrictions as may be specified in such agreement, will have any legal effect.
perturbation.justification: The original text refers to "Evaluation Materials" and "Possible Transaction". The changed text alters these terms to "Evaluation Data" and "Future Deal", respectively. The contradicted text refers to "Evaluation Materials" and "Possible Transaction", which now conflicts with the changed terminology in the modified section, leading to ambiguity. This is a Misaligned Terminology - In Text Contradiction because the changed terminology is used interchangeably without defining them, leading to conflicting obligations.

perturbation.original_text: This Agreement constitutes the entire agreement between the parties hereto regarding the subject matter hereof.
perturbation.changed_text: This Letter constitutes the complete understanding between the sides here regarding the topic matter here.
perturbation.explanation: Changing "Agreement" to "Letter", "entire agreement" to "complete understanding", "parties" to "sides", "subject matter" to "topic matter" dilutes the legal force. 'Understanding' is weaker than 'agreement'. These changes affect the enforceability of the contract, introducing an in-text contradiction in the rest of the document. For example in "Legal Remedy", there is a breach mentioned, which contradict "understanding". Creates an In-Text Contradiction
perturbation.contradicted_text: You understand and agree that money damages would not be a sufficient remedy for any breach of this Agreement by you or your representatives and that the Company will be entitled to specific performance and injunctive relief as remedies for any such breach. Such remedies shall not be deemed to be the exclusive remedies for a breach of this Agreement by your or your representatives but shall be in addition to all other remedies available at law or equity provided however any suit for damages shall be specifically limited to actual provable damages, and Company hereby waives any and all rights to seek consequential, punitive, treble or other compensatory damages of any types or kind.
perturbation.justification: The original text used strong legal terms like 'Agreement' and 'parties'. The changed text weakens these terms to 'Letter' and 'sides', and replaces 'entire agreement' with 'complete understanding'. This creates a contradiction because the contradicted text references a 'breach of this Agreement' and specific remedies. Since an 'understanding' is less binding than an 'agreement', it creates a contradiction regarding the enforceability and remedies, leading to an in-text contradiction, making it less certain if a breach of 'understanding' would trigger the same remedies as a breach of 'agreement'.
